title: Testing Standard Operating Procedure: screen stress test
bodyHtml:
What’s a screen stress test?

A screen stress test mimics the usage of a smartphone under a high load (like gaming or photo editing). 

Why’s this test important?

The screen stress test helps detect screen issues that could occur when the customer uses their smartphone for gaming or photo editing. It’s also one of the best ways to determine whether or not a refurbished screen is in working condition. 


Equipment, tools, and software needed

  • Heating mat 
  • coconutBattery or 3UTool to monitor the temperature of the device 
  • A testing software (PhoneCheck or Blancco) or a manual touch screen test
 

Test parameters and specification

  • Test conditions:
    • Device must reach 40 – 45 ° C
  • Parameters tested and pass / fail specs:
    • Perform usual screen tests (with something like PhoneCheck) and check for failures 


Scope

The procedure below applies to all smartphone screens that are being replaced — especially iPhone 11s (and above) that feature screens with a “transplanted IC.”
 

Test procedure

  • Place the device (screen facing down) on the heating mat.


 
  • Set the temperature to 45° C and the timer to 10 minutes.
  • Wait for 10 minutes.
  • Control the device’s temperature using coconutBattery or 3UTools.

  • Connect the device to PhoneCheck or another testing software.
  • Perform the usual screen tests or manual tests if no testing options are available.


 
  • Look for the test result.

Screens that pass the touch test will be considered operational and ready to sell.
